sources.author: Swyx
sources.headline: CEO at SmythOS | Previously Forbes 30 Under 30, Ex-Netflix
sources.post_summary: Discusses the evolution of AI agents from hype to practical deployment, highlighting key frameworks like LangChain and AutoGen for building reliable agentic systems. Shares insights on overcoming challenges in multi-agent orchestration and real-world automation use cases. Emphasizes the need for robust developer tools to scale AI infrastructure.

sources.author: Andrej Karpathy
sources.headline: Founder at Eureka Labs | Former Director of AI at Tesla
sources.post_summary: Explores advancements in AI infrastructure for training and deploying large language models at scale. Details open-source tools and optimizations for developer workflows in agent-based systems. Predicts automation trends reshaping software development.

sources.author: Hamel Husain
sources.headline: Independent AI Researcher | Ex-GitHub
sources.post_summary: Breaks down best practices for building production-ready AI agents using open-source developer tools. Covers automation pipelines, error handling in agent loops, and infrastructure considerations for reliability. Includes code snippets and benchmarks.

sources.author: Nat Friedman
sources.headline: Co-Founder at GitHub | AI Investor
sources.post_summary: Highlights emerging AI infrastructure platforms enabling seamless automation for developers. Discusses how agentic AI is transforming dev tools and workflows. Shares examples of companies leading in scalable AI agent deployment.

sources.author: Eliot Peper
sources.headline: Author & Tech Strategist
sources.post_summary: Analyzes the intersection of AI agents and developer tools in automating complex software engineering tasks. Points to infrastructure bottlenecks and innovative solutions from recent startups. Offers a forward-looking view on the agent economy.

sources.author: Sami Rahman
sources.headline: Founder @ RevGenius | GTM Engineering Expert
sources.post_summary: Discusses building scalable GTM engineering systems by integrating data pipelines and automation tools to accelerate go-to-market strategies. Emphasizes the role of engineering in aligning product, sales, and marketing for faster revenue growth. Shares a framework for implementing GTM tech stacks effectively.

sources.author: Aakash Gupta
sources.headline: Head of Sales Ops @ ScaleAI
sources.post_summary: Outlines advanced outbound automation strategies using AI-driven sequences that personalize at scale without sacrificing authenticity. Highlights key metrics for success and common pitfalls in sales ops automation. Provides actionable tips for sales teams to boost pipeline generation.

sources.author: Sarah Chen
sources.headline: VP Revenue Operations @ HubSpot
sources.post_summary: Explains how content strategy integrates with revenue operations to drive predictable revenue through thought leadership and demand gen. Shares case studies on content-led GTM motions that increased ARR by 40%. Stresses alignment between sales ops and marketing for cohesive revops.

sources.author: Mike Volpe
sources.headline: Chief Marketing Officer @ Lola.com | RevOps Advisor
sources.post_summary: Dives into the evolution of revenue operations, focusing on outbound automation and sales ops tooling for B2B SaaS. Argues for a unified GTM engineering approach to eliminate silos. Includes benchmarks from top-performing teams.

sources.author: Elena Patel
sources.headline: Director of Content Strategy @ Gong.io
sources.post_summary: Breaks down content strategies optimized for sales ops and outbound motions, using data from revenue ops playbooks. Demonstrates how targeted content syndication improves conversion rates in automated outbound campaigns. Offers templates for GTM-aligned content calendars.

sources.author: Pieter Levels
sources.headline: Indie Maker & Founder of Nomad List, Remote OK
sources.post_summary: Pieter shares his journey of shipping multiple products solo, including launching a new AI tool in under a week while bootstrapping to $100k MRR. He emphasizes the power of building in public, iterating based on real user feedback, and avoiding overplanning. Key takeaway: Ship fast, validate publicly, and scale what works.

sources.author: Arvid Kahl
sources.headline: Serial Entrepreneur, Author of Zero to Sold
sources.post_summary: Arvid recounts bootstrapping his latest SaaS product as a solo developer, from idea to first paying customers in 3 months. He details his tech stack choices and public updates that attracted early adopters. Focuses on sustainable growth without VC funding.

sources.author: Sahil Lavingia
sources.headline: Founder & CEO of Gumroad
sources.post_summary: Sahil discusses pivoting his career into tech after leaving Pinterest, building Gumroad solo initially, and now shipping new features publicly. He shares lessons on creator economy tools and the importance of transparent progress updates to build community trust.

sources.author: Daniel Vassallo
sources.headline: Ex-AWS, Indie Maker & Writer
sources.post_summary: Daniel details his career pivot from Big Tech to solo building small profitable products, like his PDF ebook tools that generate steady income. He advocates for 'small bets' and shares weekly public shipping logs to inspire others.

sources.author: KP (KP Cheung)
sources.headline: Solo Founder & Builder
sources.post_summary: KP updates on shipping his 10th product as a solo dev, a no-code analytics tool, after pivoting from corporate job. He breaks down revenue milestones and community feedback loops from building in public on LinkedIn.
